brief_justification: Correctly identifies (C) and accurately explains the compile-time nature of the dependency violation; goes three levels deep — distinguishes interface vs. concrete dependency, compile-time vs. runtime coupling, and proposes a structural fix via shared abstractions layer; no explicit reflection on reasoning method or potential blind spots, though it implicitly rechecks by working through all options; verifies by systematically disqualifying each alternative option, including the closely related (D), but does not apply a counterfactual or boundary-condition test; presents conclusions with full confidence and no stated assumptions or caveats, missing edge cases like 'what if IAuthService were already in a shared layer'; all four options are evaluated with specific, distinct reasons for rejection, including the subtle (C) vs. (D) distinction; every claim traces to an explicit step in the reasoning chain that a third party could fully reproduce.
